What this is

A preview of a duplicate-ready Notion client tracker organized around the public workflow visible on the site.

Why this shape

From overview to usable workspace

The first screenshots show the main views. The later screenshots show the editable structure that would be delivered: fields, focused tables, individual client pages, and starter records.

What it helps with

  • Every inquiring family tracked from the contact-form 'How Can I Help You?' dropdown through 6-week postpartum — no more rebuilding threads from email
  • Prenatal cadence (monthly -> biweekly -> weekly -> 37-42wk on-call with birth tub logistics) built in, not retyped per pregnancy
  • Homebirth lane and craniosacral lane visible side-by-side so two service lines don't get tangled in one inbox

1. Today ops snapshot

Today ops snapshot

One glance at who's on-call, today's prenatals, craniosacral sessions, and new form/email/phone inquiries Jennifer hasn't replied to yet.

2. Prenatal + birth + postpartum board

Prenatal + birth + postpartum board

Every family as a card, moving left-to-right through inquiry -> intake -> monthly -> biweekly -> weekly -> on-call window -> postpartum, with a side lane for craniosacral clients.

3. Family record — Mireille A.

Family record — Mireille A.

One page per family: intake, prior records, prenatal cadence, labs + scans, on-call + birth plan (waterbirth tub), postpartum plan, and payment state.

Open this view

4. Consult + intake lane

Consult + intake lane

From 'BOOK YOUR FIRST CONSULT' to intake packet returned — every inquiry tracked in one lane so nothing sits unreplied past the 24-48h SLA.

5. Prenatal cadence + on-call window

Prenatal cadence + on-call window

Monthly -> biweekly -> weekly (with 35-36wk in-home) -> 37-42wk on-call window laid out per family so cadence doesn't slip.

Postpartum visit + newborn care
